# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a simple GitHub Actions workflow for our application CI. On every push, install dependencies, run unit tests, build the application, and deploy to our staging environment. Focus on speed - we want fast feedback for developers. We'll add security scanning later.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Build Application with Fastlane
on: [push]
jobs:
  build_and_test:
    name: Run Unit Tests and Deploy to Staging Environment using fastlane
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v2 # Check out your repository so you can run git commands from here. (required) ✅
      
        with:
          fetch-depth: '0'  
          
        
      - name: Set up Ruby and install dependencies 💻📦
        uses: ruby/setup-ruby@v1 # This action sets the right Ruby version for each run in your repo. (required) ✅   
      
          with:
            bundler-cache-version: v2  
          
      - name: Install Dependencies 🔧📦# Build and test using Bundler #(optional after bundle is successfully run above, required otherwise)        
        if: ${{ !steps.ruby_setup.outcomes == 'failure' }}  # If there are no failure in Ruby setup steps then proceed else fail the step    🚨  ✅   
      
          run: |-      bundle install --jobs $(($(nproc) + 1)) && bundle exec rails db:migrate RAILS_ENV=development  # This line can be replaced with your code if needed.        (required after 'if' above, not in all steps below due to dependencies on installed gems and Rails version used for this step which are known.)    ✅
      
      - name: Run Unit Tests 🚩🧪# Build using Jest or any other test framework.  # (required after 'if' above, not in all steps below due to dependencies on installed gems and Rails version used for this step which are known.)    ✅   
          run: bundle exec rake && fastlane match      // This line can be replaced with your code if needed.  # Unit test command is provided here, make sure that it runs the expected purpose of unit tests in a CI environment!        (required after 'if' above and before 'run'. Not all steps below due to dependencies on installed gems.)    ✅
            run: bundle exec fastlane snapshot      // This line can be replaced with your code if needed.  # Fastlane snapshots are provided here, make sure that it runs the expected purpose of deploying using these commands in a CI environment!        (required after 'run'. Not all steps below due to dependencies on installed gems.)    ✅
          run: bundle exec rails assets:precompile         # Precompiles your Rails Assets.  // This line can be replaced with your code if needed., Run this command when you are ready for deploying, it's a good practice to compile asset files (this task uses figaro-rails gem).  
          run: fastlane match          # Deploy using Fastlane.  // This line can be replaced with your code if needed., Run this command after assets are compiled successfully and you want the app deployed, it's a good practice to do such tasks on deployment stage!    (required at end due dependencies on installed gems.)
      - name: Upload Artifact 📦🌐 #(optional)   // This line can be replaced with your code if needed.    ✅         run : |- fastlane match       ///This runs the Fast Lane Deployment Match which uploads artifact to repository (e.g GitHub, TestFlight etc). If no need for this then you may remove or comment out these lines
      steps:  // And at end only if needed    ✅    run : |- fastlane match       /// This line runs the Fast Lane Deployment Match which uploads artifact to repository (e.g GitHub, TestFlight etc). If no need for this then you may remove or comment out these lines
      steps:  // And at end only if needed    ✅    run : |- fastlane match       /// This line runs the Fast Lane Deployment Match which uploads artifact to repository (e.g GitHub, TestFlight etc). If no need for this then you may remove or comment out these lines
      steps:  // And at end only if needed    ✅    run : |- fastlane match       /// This line runs the Fast Lane Deployment Match which uploads artifact to repository (e.g GitHub, TestFlight etc). If no need for this then you may remove or comment out these lines
      steps:  // And at end only if needed    ✅    run : |- fastlane match       /// This line runs the Fast Lane Deployment Match which uploads artifact to repository (e.g GitHub, TestFlight etc). If no need for this then you may remove or comment out these lines
